taskPreset.title: Create Ponytail Task
taskPreset.description: Queue a structured Ponytail task preset with command-based mode selection, repository scope, and skill-first execution.

panel.projectHint: The Ponytail task will be created in the selected project.
panel.taskNameHint: Optional. Leave empty to let the title be optimized asynchronously after dispatch.
panel.repositoryAccessHint: Choose repositories from the selected project and assign read or write access per repository.
panel.repositoryAccessEmpty: No repositories are available for the selected project yet.
panel.commandHint: Choose the Ponytail mode or tool to run. `full` is the recommended default command.
panel.queryHint: Describe the task, code target, audit scope, or help request Ponytail should handle.
panel.queryPlaceholder: Build a date range filter for the dashboard without over-engineering it.

lite.description: Gentle guidance. Build what was asked, but name the lazier alternative in one line.

full.description: Default. Enforce the ladder, prefer stdlib and native features, and keep the diff short.

ultra.description: Ruthless minimum. Challenge extra scope and prefer deletion or a one-liner whenever possible.

review.description: Review code or a diff for over-engineering, unnecessary abstractions, and obvious simplifications.

audit.description: Scan a repository or project scope for bloat, dead flexibility, and stdlib or native replacements.

debt.description: Collect `ponytail:` shortcuts into a debt ledger and distinguish deliberate deferrals from real complexity.

help.description: Show the Ponytail quick reference for modes, triggers, skills, and usage patterns.
